description: BLANK WHEN ZERO: numeric field displays as spaces when value is zero
notes: IBM: BLANK WHEN ZERO replaces display output with spaces when value is 0. Aletheia: blank_when_zero flag is set on CobolDecimal, to_display() returns spaces. However PIC 9(5) is parsed as pic_integers=1 (PIC regex truncated by BLANK keyword). MOVE uses to_display() which returns 1 space, then ljust(5) pads to 5 spaces. Net result matches IBM for the MOVE target. WS-AMT internal .value remains Decimal('0'). GAP: PIC digit count lost when BLANK WHEN ZERO follows PIC clause.
